title: Production-optimized greenhouse
abstract: A production-optimized greenhouse comprises a floor slab with a forward edge thereof facing the direction of desired maximum sun exposure for the greenhouse; a planar front wall extending upwards from a lower forward edge anchored to the floor slab in alignment with the forward edge of the slab and including at least partial translucent material on the planar surface thereof to allow diffused entry of exterior light into the interior growing area; a non-translucent convex arcuate back wall adjoining an upper edge of the front wall and extending rearwardly and downward therefrom to a lower rear edge anchored to the floor slab; and non-translucent end walls. Optional controlled sunshades permit the control of light ingress to the growing area dependent upon the positioning of the sun, and the configuration of the building is optimized for use in cold weather and similar extremes
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A production-optimized greenhouse for use on a building support surface, said greenhouse defining an interior growing area and comprising: a. a floor slab resting in position on the building support surface, with a forward edge thereof facing the direction of desired maximum sun exposure for the greenhouse; b. a planar front wall having an outer surface facing the exterior environment and an interior surface facing into the interior growing area, said front wall extending upwards from a lower forward edge anchored to the floor slab in alignment with the forward edge of the slab and including at least partial translucent material on the planar surface thereof to allow diffused entry of exterior light into the interior growing area; c. a non-trans lucent convex arcuate back wall adjoining an upper edge of the front wall and extending rearwardly and downward therefrom to a lower rear edge anchored to the floor slab, said backwall having a reflective interior surface finish; and d. non-translucent end walls, at least one of which includes at least one entry door for entrance to the interior growing area from exterior environment. SUBSTITUTE SHEET (RULE 26) 2. The greenhouse of Claim 1 wherein the translucent material in the front wall comprises: a. a plurality of windows arrayed and mounted therein; or b. translucent sheathing material attached to the wall structure. 3. The greenhouse of Claim 1 further comprising interior walls extending from the front wall to the lower rear edge of the back wall and matching the interior of the curvature of the back wall, creating environmentally segregated subdivisions of the interior growing area. 4. The greenhouse of Claim 1 wherein the back wall consists of a steel Quonset-style panel wall. 5. The greenhouse of Claim 3 wherein: a. the back wall consists of a steel Quonset-style panel wall; and b. the interior walls are capped along their curved edge with a manufactured mating edge portion shaped to engage the curvature of the back wall as defined by the interior of the panels of the back wall. SUBSTITUTE SHEET (RULE 26) 6. The greenhouse of Claim 1 further comprising adjustable sunscreens to permit adjustment of the exposed translucent area of the front wall and allow control of the amount of light entering the interior growing area. 7. The greenhouse of Claim 6 wherein the adjustable sunscreens are automated and operate in conjunction with a sensor of the positioning and level of available sunlight, whereby the automated opening or closing of the sunscreens can normalize or maximize sunlight within the interior growing area based on the horizon positioning of the sun. 8. The greenhouse of Claim 1 wherein the back wall or the end walls are insulated to enhance cold weather utility of the greenhouse. 9. The greenhouse of Claim 1 wherein the back wall comprises material capable of being embedded in a hill or other earth works. 10. The greenhouse of Claim 1 wherein the back wall comprises a flexible membrane. 11. The greenhouse of Claim 1 wherein the reflective interior surface finish of the back wall comprises white surface coating. SUBSTITUTE SHEET (RULE 26)
